summaryText: Guest Speaker Scott Williams opened with energetic greetings and a humorous story about Billy Graham to introduce the central theme of proximity to Jesus. He taught from Mark 2:1-12, the story of the paralyzed man lowered through a roof by his friends. Pastor Scott framed this as a message about 'The Power of Proximity,' explaining that our spiritual breakthrough and identity are directly tied to how close we are to the right influences. He emphasized that people often become defined by their condition—like being 'paralyzed' by sin, addiction, or negative thought patterns—but Jesus offers forgiveness and freedom. The sermon outlined three key areas for intentional proximity: being around the right people who will carry you to Christ and hold you accountable, positioning yourself in the right places like the house of God and life-giving community, and prioritizing the right things, which are the things of Jesus such as serving, giving, and sharing faith. Pastor Scott wove in personal stories from his time as a prison warden and parole board member to illustrate how changing one's environment and connections leads to transformation. He concluded with a powerful altar call, inviting those present to surrender their lives to Christ and commit to a life of close proximity to Him, assuring them that this is the key to overcoming the 'giants' they face.
mainPoints: Proximity to the right people is essential for spiritual growth and breakthrough; we need a 'core four' who will carry us to Jesus and hold us accountable.
Proximity to the right places, like the house of God and faith communities, positions us to run the right races and receive God's blessing.
Proximity to the right things means prioritizing the things of Jesus—serving, giving, and sharing the gospel—which unlocks doors of blessing and redefines us from our conditions.

versesMentioned.text: A few days later, when Jesus again entered Capernaum, the people heard that he had come home. They gathered in such large numbers that there was no room left, not even outside the door, and he preached the word to them. Some men came, bringing to him a paralyzed man, carried by four of them. Since they could not get him to Jesus because of the crowd, they made an opening in the roof above Jesus by digging through it and then lowered the mat the man was lying on. When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the paralyzed man, 'Son, your sins are forgiven.' Now some teachers of the law were sitting there, thinking to themselves, 'Why does this fellow talk like that? He’s blaspheming! Who can forgive sins but God alone?' Immediately Jesus knew in his spirit that this was what they were thinking in their hearts, and he said to them, 'Why are you thinking these things? Which is easier: to say to this paralyzed man, ‘Your sins are forgiven,’ or to say, ‘Get up, take your mat and walk’? But I want you to know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins.' So he said to the man, 'I tell you, get up, take your mat and go home.' He got up, took his mat and walked out in full view of them all. This amazed everyone and they praised God, saying, 'We have never seen anything like this!'

Monday.scripture: Mark 2:3-4 - 'Some men came, bringing to him a paralyzed man, carried by four of them. Since they could not get him to Jesus because of the crowd, they made an opening in the roof above Jesus by digging through it and then lowered the mat the man was lying on.'
Monday.action: Identify one person in your life who encourages your faith. Send them a text to thank them and commit to being that kind of friend to someone else.
Monday.prayer: Lord, thank you for the people who carry me closer to You. Help me to be a faithful friend and to seek out relationships that honor You.

Tuesday.scripture: Mark 2:1-2 - '...the people heard that he had come home. They gathered in such large numbers that there was no room left, not even outside the door, and he preached the word to them.'
Tuesday.action: Make a concrete plan to be in church this coming Sunday. If you are part of a small group, reach out to the leader; if not, explore joining one.
Tuesday.prayer: God, draw me to the places where Your presence is strong. Give me a hunger to be in Your house and in community with other believers.

Wednesday.scripture: Mark 2:5 - 'When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the paralyzed man, ‘Son, your sins are forgiven.’'
Wednesday.action: Spend 10 minutes in prayer, specifically asking God to increase your faith and to show you one area of your life where you need to trust Him more.
Wednesday.prayer: Jesus, I believe; help my unbelief. Forgive my sins and increase my faith to see You move in my life today.

Thursday.scripture: Mark 2:9-11 - 'Which is easier: to say to this paralyzed man, ‘Your sins are forgiven,’ or to say, ‘Get up, take your mat and walk’? ...He said to the man, ‘I tell you, get up, take your mat and go home.’'
Thursday.action: Write down one 'mat'—a past failure, sin, or label—that you have been carrying. In prayer, symbolically lay it down and accept Christ's forgiveness and new identity for you.
Thursday.prayer: Father, I receive Your forgiveness today. I lay down the weight of my past and choose to walk in the freedom and new identity You give me.

Friday.scripture: Mark 2:12 - 'He got up, took his mat and walked out in full view of them all. This amazed everyone and they praised God...'
Friday.action: Share a story of God's goodness in your life with one person today, whether in person or through a social media post. Let your testimony praise God.
Friday.prayer: Holy Spirit, give me courage to share what You have done for me. Use my story to amaze others and lead them to praise You.

Saturday.scripture: 2 Corinthians 10:5 - 'We demolish arguments and every pretension that sets itself up against the knowledge of God, and we take captive every thought to make it obedient to Christ.'
Saturday.action: Practice the discipline from the sermon. When a negative, anxious, or sinful thought arises today, consciously 'capture' it and replace it with a truth from Scripture or a prayer.
Saturday.prayer: Lord, I submit my mind to You. Help me to recognize thoughts that are not from You and to make them obedient to Christ, the author of my peace.
